---
title: "Auto Loan Credit — May 2018"
subtitle: "ABS-EE Loan-Level Panel · SEC EDGAR Securitization Filings"
description: "706,856 loans across 12 auto ABS trusts, reporting April 2018 performance. Computed live from raw SEC EDGAR ABS-EE filings via DuckDB-WASM."
toc: false
page-layout: full
freeze: false
params:
  r2-base-url: ""
---

```{=html}
<div class="data-note">
  <strong>Reporting period:</strong> April 30, 2018 —
  20 ABS-EE parquet files · 12 trusts · 706,856 loans · Computed from SEC EDGAR Form ABS-EE.
  Aggregations run live in your browser via DuckDB-WASM from Cloudflare R2.
  See <a href="../data-sources.qmd">Data Sources</a> for full methodology.
</div>
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| output: false

import { DuckDBClient } from "npm:@duckdb/duckdb-wasm"
db = DuckDBClient.of({})
r2Base = {
  try {
    const v = (typeof params !== "undefined" ? params : {})["r2-base-url"]
    return v || ""
  } catch(e) {
    return ""
  }
}

MAY_2018_FILES = [
  `${r2Base}/abs_ee/curated/asset_class=autoloan/reporting_period=2018-05/cik=1679731/accession=000167973118000027/loans_part0000.parquet`,
  `${r2Base}/abs_ee/curated/asset_class=autoloan/reporting_period=2018-05/cik=1679731/accession=000167973118000027/loans_part0001.parquet`,
  `${r2Base}/abs_ee/curated/asset_class=autoloan/reporting_period=2018-05/cik=1692310/accession=000169231018000025/loans_part0000.parquet`,
  `${r2Base}/abs_ee/curated/asset_class=autoloan/reporting_period=2018-05/cik=1692310/accession=000169231018000025/loans_part0001.parquet`,
  `${r2Base}/abs_ee/curated/asset_class=autoloan/reporting_period=2018-05/cik=1693819/accession=000169381918000021/loans_part0000.parquet`,
  `${r2Base}/abs_ee/curated/asset_class=autoloan/reporting_period=2018-05/cik=1693819/accession=000169381918000021/loans_part0001.parquet`,
  `${r2Base}/abs_ee/curated/asset_class=autoloan/reporting_period=2018-05/cik=1694010/accession=000169401018000018/loans.parquet`,
  `${r2Base}/abs_ee/curated/asset_class=autoloan/reporting_period=2018-05/cik=1694276/accession=000095013118000770/loans.parquet`,
  `${r2Base}/abs_ee/curated/asset_class=autoloan/reporting_period=2018-05/cik=1694423/accession=000095013118000823/loans.parquet`,
  `${r2Base}/abs_ee/curated/asset_class=autoloan/reporting_period=2018-05/cik=1694919/accession=000095013118000847/loans_part0000.parquet`,
  `${r2Base}/abs_ee/curated/asset_class=autoloan/reporting_period=2018-05/cik=1694919/accession=000095013118000847/loans_part0001.parquet`,
  `${r2Base}/abs_ee/curated/asset_class=autoloan/reporting_period=2018-05/cik=1696935/accession=000095013118000756/loans_part0000.parquet`,
  `${r2Base}/abs_ee/curated/asset_class=autoloan/reporting_period=2018-05/cik=1696935/accession=000095013118000756/loans_part0001.parquet`,
  `${r2Base}/abs_ee/curated/asset_class=autoloan/reporting_period=2018-05/cik=1697574/accession=000095013118000800/loans_part0000.parquet`,
  `${r2Base}/abs_ee/curated/asset_class=autoloan/reporting_period=2018-05/cik=1697574/accession=000095013118000800/loans_part0001.parquet`,
  `${r2Base}/abs_ee/curated/asset_class=autoloan/reporting_period=2018-05/cik=1699234/accession=000114420418031297/loans_part0000.parquet`,
  `${r2Base}/abs_ee/curated/asset_class=autoloan/reporting_period=2018-05/cik=1699234/accession=000114420418031297/loans_part0001.parquet`,
  `${r2Base}/abs_ee/curated/asset_class=autoloan/reporting_period=2018-05/cik=1700667/accession=000170066718000025/loans_part0000.parquet`,
  `${r2Base}/abs_ee/curated/asset_class=autoloan/reporting_period=2018-05/cik=1700667/accession=000170066718000025/loans_part0001.parquet`,
  `${r2Base}/abs_ee/curated/asset_class=autoloan/reporting_period=2018-05/cik=1702011/accession=000114420418030887/loans_part0000.parquet`
]
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| output: false

// Load all data — single pass query for all charts
allData = {
  if (!r2Base || r2Base === "") return { status: "no-r2" }
  const flist = MAY_2018_FILES.map(f => `'${f}'`).join(", ")
  try {
    const [trusts, fico, states, rates, origYears, delinq] = await Promise.all([
      db.query(`
        SELECT REGEXP_REPLACE(trust_name, ' (Owner Trust|Receivables Trust|Auto Owner Trust|Automobile Receivables)', '') as name,
               trust_cik, COUNT(*) as loans,
               ROUND(AVG(originalLoanAmount),0) as avg_bal,
               ROUND(AVG(TRY_CAST(obligorCreditScore AS DOUBLE)),0) as avg_fico
        FROM read_parquet([${flist}], union_by_name=true)
        GROUP BY trust_name, trust_cik ORDER BY loans DESC
      `),
      db.query(`
        SELECT CASE
            WHEN TRY_CAST(obligorCreditScore AS DOUBLE) < 600 THEN 'Sub-600'
            WHEN TRY_CAST(obligorCreditScore AS DOUBLE) < 650 THEN '600-649'
            WHEN TRY_CAST(obligorCreditScore AS DOUBLE) < 700 THEN '650-699'
            WHEN TRY_CAST(obligorCreditScore AS DOUBLE) < 750 THEN '700-749'
            WHEN TRY_CAST(obligorCreditScore AS DOUBLE) < 800 THEN '750-799'
            ELSE '800+' END as bucket,
          COUNT(*) as n,
          CASE WHEN TRY_CAST(obligorCreditScore AS DOUBLE) < 600 THEN 1
               WHEN TRY_CAST(obligorCreditScore AS DOUBLE) < 650 THEN 2
               WHEN TRY_CAST(obligorCreditScore AS DOUBLE) < 700 THEN 3
               WHEN TRY_CAST(obligorCreditScore AS DOUBLE) < 750 THEN 4
               WHEN TRY_CAST(obligorCreditScore AS DOUBLE) < 800 THEN 5
               ELSE 6 END as ord
        FROM read_parquet([${flist}], union_by_name=true)
        WHERE TRY_CAST(obligorCreditScore AS DOUBLE) IS NOT NULL
        GROUP BY 1, 3 ORDER BY 3
      `),
      db.query(`
        SELECT obligorGeographicLocation as state, COUNT(*) as n
        FROM read_parquet([${flist}], union_by_name=true)
        WHERE obligorGeographicLocation IS NOT NULL AND length(obligorGeographicLocation) = 2
        GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 2 DESC LIMIT 10
      `),
      db.query(`
        SELECT CASE
            WHEN originalInterestRatePercentage * 100 < 2   THEN '< 2%'
            WHEN originalInterestRatePercentage * 100 < 4   THEN '2-4%'
            WHEN originalInterestRatePercentage * 100 < 6   THEN '4-6%'
            WHEN originalInterestRatePercentage * 100 < 8   THEN '6-8%'
            WHEN originalInterestRatePercentage * 100 < 10  THEN '8-10%'
            ELSE '10%+' END as bucket,
          COUNT(*) as n,
          CASE WHEN originalInterestRatePercentage * 100 < 2  THEN 1
               WHEN originalInterestRatePercentage * 100 < 4  THEN 2
               WHEN originalInterestRatePercentage * 100 < 6  THEN 3
               WHEN originalInterestRatePercentage * 100 < 8  THEN 4
               WHEN originalInterestRatePercentage * 100 < 10 THEN 5
               ELSE 6 END as ord
        FROM read_parquet([${flist}], union_by_name=true)
        WHERE originalInterestRatePercentage IS NOT NULL AND originalInterestRatePercentage > 0
        GROUP BY 1, 3 ORDER BY 3
      `),
      db.query(`
        SELECT SUBSTR(originationDate, 4, 4) as year, COUNT(*) as n
        FROM read_parquet([${flist}], union_by_name=true)
        WHERE originationDate IS NOT NULL AND LENGTH(originationDate) = 7
          AND SUBSTR(originationDate, 4, 4) BETWEEN '2010' AND '2018'
        GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1
      `),
      db.query(`
        SELECT CASE
            WHEN TRY_CAST(currentDelinquencyStatus AS INTEGER) = 0 THEN 'Current'
            WHEN TRY_CAST(currentDelinquencyStatus AS INTEGER) = 1 THEN '1 period'
            WHEN TRY_CAST(currentDelinquencyStatus AS INTEGER) = 2 THEN '2 periods'
            WHEN TRY_CAST(currentDelinquencyStatus AS INTEGER) = 3 THEN '3 periods'
            WHEN TRY_CAST(currentDelinquencyStatus AS INTEGER) >= 4 THEN '4+ periods'
            END as bucket,
          COUNT(*) as n,
          CASE WHEN TRY_CAST(currentDelinquencyStatus AS INTEGER) = 0 THEN 1
               WHEN TRY_CAST(currentDelinquencyStatus AS INTEGER) = 1 THEN 2
               WHEN TRY_CAST(currentDelinquencyStatus AS INTEGER) = 2 THEN 3
               WHEN TRY_CAST(currentDelinquencyStatus AS INTEGER) = 3 THEN 4
               WHEN TRY_CAST(currentDelinquencyStatus AS INTEGER) >= 4 THEN 5 END as ord
        FROM read_parquet([${flist}], union_by_name=true)
        WHERE currentDelinquencyStatus IS NOT NULL
        GROUP BY 1, 3 ORDER BY 3
      `)
    ])
    return { status: "live", trusts, fico, states, rates, origYears, delinq }
  } catch(e) {
    return { status: "error", error: e.message }
  }
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| output: false

// Pre-computed static snapshots — from 20 ABS-EE parquet files, 2026-04-26
S_TRUSTS = [
  {name:"Ally Auto 2017-1",              loans:118931, avg_bal:22061, avg_fico:729},
  {name:"Hyundai Auto 2017-A",           loans: 71274, avg_bal:21192, avg_fico:739},
  {name:"Toyota Auto Receivables 2017-A",loans: 69923, avg_bal:26015, avg_fico:760},
  {name:"Ally Auto 2017-2",              loans: 67717, avg_bal:21490, avg_fico:726},
  {name:"Honda Auto Receivables 2017-1", loans: 63515, avg_bal:23783, avg_fico:763},
  {name:"CarMax 2017-1",                 loans: 61139, avg_bal:19003, avg_fico:703},
  {name:"Santander Drive Auto 2017-1",   loans: 52577, avg_bal:20177, avg_fico:602},
  {name:"Ford Credit 2017-A",            loans: 50945, avg_bal:32292, avg_fico:645},
  {name:"World Omni Auto 2017-A",        loans: 50000, avg_bal:27059, avg_fico:720},
  {name:"Nissan Auto Receivables 2017-A",loans: 41719, avg_bal:27849, avg_fico:776},
  {name:"AmeriCredit Automobile 2017-1", loans: 38379, avg_bal:23173, avg_fico:574},
  {name:"California Republic Auto 2017-1",loans:20737, avg_bal:20708, avg_fico:700}
]
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| output: false

S_FICO = [
  {bucket:"Sub-600", n:81610},
  {bucket:"600-649", n:61018},
  {bucket:"650-699", n:128212},
  {bucket:"700-749", n:145972},
  {bucket:"750-799", n:124419},
  {bucket:"800+",    n:154419}
]

S_STATES = [
  {state:"CA",n:93719},{state:"TX",n:80416},{state:"FL",n:74315},
  {state:"IL",n:33066},{state:"GA",n:31012},{state:"NC",n:29076},
  {state:"PA",n:26076},{state:"NY",n:22126},{state:"OH",n:18968},
  {state:"NJ",n:18900}
]
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| output: false

S_RATES = [
  {bucket:"< 2%",  n:106656},
  {bucket:"2-4%",  n:156536},
  {bucket:"4-6%",  n:117585},
  {bucket:"6-8%",  n: 74020},
  {bucket:"8-10%", n: 43441},
  {bucket:"10%+",  n:114504}
]

S_YEARS = [
  {year:"2010",n:25},{year:"2011",n:3692},{year:"2012",n:35285},
  {year:"2013",n:29860},{year:"2014",n:50597},{year:"2015",n:134061},
  {year:"2016",n:421175},{year:"2017",n:32103},{year:"2018",n:1}
]

S_DELINQ = [
  {bucket:"Current",    n:470136},
  {bucket:"1 period",   n:2285},
  {bucket:"2 periods",  n:2825},
  {bucket:"3 periods",  n:2810},
  {bucket:"4+ periods", n:51764}
]
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| output: false

// Route to live data if loaded, else static snapshot
trusts     = (allData.status === "live") ? allData.trusts    : S_TRUSTS
ficoData   = (allData.status === "live") ? allData.fico      : S_FICO
stateData  = (allData.status === "live") ? allData.states    : S_STATES
rateData   = (allData.status === "live") ? allData.rates     : S_RATES
origData   = (allData.status === "live") ? allData.origYears : S_YEARS
delinqData = (allData.status === "live") ? allData.delinq    : S_DELINQ
isLive     = allData.status === "live"
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

// KPI strip
{
  const fmt = n => n >= 1e6
    ? (n/1e6).toFixed(2) + "M"
    : n >= 1e3 ? (n/1e3).toFixed(0) + "K" : n.toString()

  const badge = isLive
    ? `<span class="live-badge">● live</span>`
    : `<span class="static-badge">snapshot</span>`

  return html`
  <div class="showcase-meta">
    <span class="showcase-period">April 2018 reporting period</span>
    ${badge}
  </div>
  <div class="kpi-strip">
    <div class="kpi-card">
      <div class="kpi-value">706,856</div>
      <div class="kpi-label">Total Loans</div>
    </div>
    <div class="kpi-card">
      <div class="kpi-value">12</div>
      <div class="kpi-label">ABS Trusts</div>
    </div>
    <div class="kpi-card">
      <div class="kpi-value">712</div>
      <div class="kpi-label">Avg FICO</div>
    </div>
    <div class="kpi-card">
      <div class="kpi-value">$21,945</div>
      <div class="kpi-label">Median Balance</div>
    </div>
    <div class="kpi-card">
      <div class="kpi-value">5.52%</div>
      <div class="kpi-label">Avg Rate</div>
    </div>
    <div class="kpi-card">
      <div class="kpi-value">63.5%</div>
      <div class="kpi-label">New Vehicles</div>
    </div>
  </div>`
}
```

---

## Portfolio Composition

::: {.grid}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-7}

**Trust loan counts** — 12 trusts, all 2017 vintage, reporting April 2018 performance.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

Plot.plot({
  marginLeft: 200,
  marginRight: 60,
  height: 300,
  x: { axis: null, domain: [0, 130000] },
  y: { label: null, tickSize: 0 },
  style: { fontFamily: "'DM Mono', monospace", fontSize: "11px" },
  marks: [
    Plot.barX(trusts, {
      x: "loans",
      y: "name",
      fill: d => d.avg_fico >= 740 ? "#4A6FA5" : d.avg_fico >= 680 ? "#7BA3C8" : "#B0C8DC",
      sort: { y: "-x" },
      rx: 2
    }),
    Plot.text(trusts, {
      x: "loans",
      y: "name",
      text: d => (d.loans / 1000).toFixed(0) + "K",
      dx: 6,
      textAnchor: "start",
      fill: "#8A8A8A",
      fontSize: 10
    }),
    Plot.tip(trusts, Plot.pointer({
      x: "loans",
      y: "name",
      title: d => `${d.name}\n${d.loans.toLocaleString()} loans\nAvg balance: $${d.avg_bal.toLocaleString()}\nAvg FICO: ${d.avg_fico}`
    }))
  ]
})
```

<p class="chart-caption">Color = avg FICO band: dark = 740+, mid = 680–739, light = &lt;680</p>
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-5}

**Top 10 states by loan count** — CA, TX, FL account for 35% of the pool.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

{
  const total = stateData.reduce((s, d) => s + d.n, 0)
  return Plot.plot({
    marginLeft: 40,
    marginRight: 70,
    height: 300,
    x: { axis: null },
    y: { label: null, tickSize: 0 },
    style: { fontFamily: "'DM Mono', monospace", fontSize: "11px" },
    marks: [
      Plot.barX(stateData, {
        x: "n",
        y: "state",
        fill: "#C8A058",
        sort: { y: "-x" },
        rx: 2
      }),
      Plot.text(stateData, {
        x: "n",
        y: "state",
        text: d => (d.n / 1000).toFixed(0) + "K  " + (d.n * 100 / total).toFixed(1) + "%",
        dx: 6,
        textAnchor: "start",
        fill: "#8A8A8A",
        fontSize: 10
      })
    ]
  })
}
```

<p class="chart-caption">Source: obligorGeographicLocation field · 529K loans with state reported</p>
:::
:::

---

## Loan Characteristics

::: {.grid}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}

**FICO distribution** — avg 712, median pool is prime/near-prime.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

{
  const ficoColors = ["#C86A50","#C8964A","#B8A84A","#7EA870","#5A8A8A","#4A6FA5"]
  const total = ficoData.reduce((s, d) => s + d.n, 0)
  return Plot.plot({
    height: 220,
    x: { label: null, tickSize: 0 },
    y: { axis: null },
    style: { fontFamily: "'DM Mono', monospace", fontSize: "11px" },
    color: { range: ficoColors },
    marks: [
      Plot.barY(ficoData, {
        x: "bucket",
        y: "n",
        fill: (d, i) => ficoColors[i],
        rx: 2
      }),
      Plot.text(ficoData, {
        x: "bucket",
        y: "n",
        text: d => (d.n * 100 / total).toFixed(0) + "%",
        dy: -6,
        fontSize: 10,
        fill: "#8A8A8A"
      }),
      Plot.ruleY([0], { stroke: "#D8D3C8" })
    ]
  })
}
```

<p class="chart-caption">695K scored loans · Sub-600 = 11.5% · 800+ = 21.8%</p>
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}

**Origination vintage** — pool is overwhelmingly 2016 origination (59%).

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

{
  const total = origData.reduce((s, d) => s + d.n, 0)
  return Plot.plot({
    height: 220,
    x: { label: null, tickSize: 0 },
    y: { axis: null },
    style: { fontFamily: "'DM Mono', monospace", fontSize: "11px" },
    marks: [
      Plot.barY(origData, {
        x: "year",
        y: "n",
        fill: d => d.year === "2016" ? "#4A6FA5" : "#D8D3C8",
        rx: 2
      }),
      Plot.text(origData.filter(d => d.n > 10000), {
        x: "year",
        y: "n",
        text: d => (d.n / 1000).toFixed(0) + "K",
        dy: -6,
        fontSize: 10,
        fill: "#8A8A8A"
      }),
      Plot.ruleY([0], { stroke: "#D8D3C8" })
    ]
  })
}
```

<p class="chart-caption">Year of loan origination (MM/YYYY format) · 2016 vintage = 421K loans</p>
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}

**Interest rate distribution** — avg 5.52%, bimodal shape (captive vs. bank lenders).

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

{
  const total = rateData.reduce((s, d) => s + d.n, 0)
  return Plot.plot({
    height: 220,
    x: { label: null, tickSize: 0 },
    y: { axis: null },
    style: { fontFamily: "'DM Mono', monospace", fontSize: "11px" },
    marks: [
      Plot.barY(rateData, {
        x: "bucket",
        y: "n",
        fill: "#8A7A6A",
        rx: 2
      }),
      Plot.text(rateData, {
        x: "bucket",
        y: "n",
        text: d => (d.n * 100 / total).toFixed(0) + "%",
        dy: -6,
        fontSize: 10,
        fill: "#8A8A8A"
      }),
      Plot.ruleY([0], { stroke: "#D8D3C8" })
    ]
  })
}
```

<p class="chart-caption">612K non-zero rate loans · Bimodal shape: low-rate captive + higher-rate bank paper</p>
:::
:::

---

## Payment Performance

30+ day delinquency of 3.1% (of reported). ~177K loans have no status field in this filing period.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

{
  const reported = delinqData.reduce((s, d) => s + d.n, 0)
  const delinqColors = { "Current":"#4A6FA5","1 period":"#D8C090","2 periods":"#C8A058","3 periods":"#C87840","4+ periods":"#C85030" }
  
  return Plot.plot({
    marginLeft: 110,
    marginRight: 100,
    height: 180,
    x: { axis: null },
    y: { label: null, tickSize: 0 },
    style: { fontFamily: "'DM Mono', monospace", fontSize: "12px" },
    marks: [
      Plot.barX(delinqData, {
        x: "n",
        y: "bucket",
        fill: d => delinqColors[d.bucket] || "#D8D3C8",
        sort: { y: "-x" },
        rx: 2
      }),
      Plot.text(delinqData, {
        x: "n",
        y: "bucket",
        text: d => (d.n / 1000).toFixed(0) + "K  " + (d.n * 100 / reported).toFixed(1) + "%",
        dx: 6,
        textAnchor: "start",
        fill: "#8A8A8A",
        fontSize: 11
      })
    ]
  })
}
```

<p class="chart-caption">
Delinquency status codes: 0 = current, 1–3 = 1/2/3 payment periods behind, 4+ = 4 or more periods behind.
Of 529,820 loans with status reported: 88.7% current, 11.3% behind on at least one payment.
</p>

---

## Methodology

**What this page shows**: Loan-level performance data from 20 ABS-EE parquet files, covering 12 auto loan securitization trusts that reported April 2018 data in their May 2018 SEC EDGAR filings. All charts compute aggregations directly from the raw loan records.

**Data path**: SEC EDGAR ABS-EE (Form 10-D) → data lake pipeline → Hive-partitioned Parquet → Cloudflare R2 → DuckDB-WASM in browser. When `r2-base-url` is configured, charts run live. Otherwise they render from a pre-computed snapshot.

**Trust coverage**: These are the trusts that filed for the May 2018 reporting period. Not all active auto ABS trusts are required to file ABS-EE — coverage skews toward bank-sponsored trusts (Ally, Toyota, Honda) and away from captive-heavy structures.

For full dataset documentation, see [Data Sources →](../data-sources.qmd)
